gold_standard.description: Gold standard for evaluating a RAG system on English Wikipedia articles (topic: literature and literary movements). Contains definition questions, sections, categories, content search, metadata, counting, existence, listings, and relationships between articles. Includes a 'cross_document' category with multi-hop questions (shared entities, bidirectional and chain references) designed to expose the limits of basic vector RAG and to be answerable by a knowledge graph. All answers computed from data.

preguntas.pregunta: What is Créolité?
preguntas.respuesta: Créolité (French pronunciation: [kʁeɔlite]) is a literary movement first developed in the 1980s by the Martinican writers Patrick Chamoiseau, Jean Bernabé and Raphaël Confiant.

preguntas.pregunta: Define the concept of Literature.
preguntas.respuesta: Literature is any collection of written work.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the article about Young Vienna about?
preguntas.respuesta: Young Vienna (Jung-Wien), also referred to as Young Austria, was a society of fin de siècle writers who met in Vienna's Café Griensteidl and other nearby coffeehouses in the late nineteenth century.

preguntas.pregunta: Briefly explain what Bizarro fiction is.
preguntas.respuesta: Bizarro fiction is a contemporary literary genre which often uses elements of absurdism, satire, and the grotesque, along with pop-surrealism and genre fiction staples, in order to create subversive, weird, and entertaining works.

preguntas.pregunta: Give me a description of Western canon.
preguntas.respuesta: The Western canon is the embodiment of high-culture literature, music, philosophy, and art that are highly cherished across the Western world, such works having achieved the status of classics.

preguntas.pregunta: What concept does the term Onirism designate?
preguntas.respuesta: Onirism was a surrealist Romanian literary school most popular during the 1960s, in the wake of popular uprisings in Eastern Europe.

preguntas.pregunta: What does Rhyming Chroniclers consist of?
preguntas.respuesta: Rhyming Chroniclers, a series of writers who flourished in England in the 13th century, and related histories of the country in rhyme, in which the fabulous occupies a conspicuous place, among which Layamon's Brut (1205) takes the lead.

preguntas.pregunta: Provide the definition of Acmeist poetry.
preguntas.respuesta: Acmeism, or the Guild of Poets, was a modernist transient poetic school, which emerged c.

preguntas.pregunta: list all pairs of articles in the corpus that share at least 3 categories in common. Provide the count and each pair with its number of shared categories.
Tell me about Romantic literature. What is it exactly?
preguntas.respuesta: In literature, Romanticism found recurrent themes in the evocation or criticism of the past, the cult of "sensibility" with its emphasis on women and children, the isolation of the artist or narrator, and respect for nature.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the meaning of Progressive Writers' Movement?
preguntas.respuesta: The Progressive Writers' Association or the Progressive Writers' Movement of India or Anjuman Tarraqi Pasand Mussanafin-e-Hind (Urdu:  انجمن ترقی پسند مصنفینِ ہند) or Akhil Bhartiya Pragatishil Lekhak Sangh (Hindi: अखिल भारतीय प्रगतिशील लेखक संघ) was a progressive literary movement in pre-partition British India.

preguntas.pregunta: If I look up Magnetic Poetry, what information do I find?
preguntas.respuesta: Magnetic Poetry is a toy and creative writing aid consisting of individual words—often related to a particular theme or topic—printed on small magnets which can be creatively arranged into poetry on a refrigerator or other metal surface.

Describe the concept covered under the name Second Sophistic.
preguntas.respuesta: The Second Sophistic is a literary-historical term referring to the Greek writers who flourished from the reign of Nero until c.

preguntas.pregunta: Which 5 articles have the most words? Provide the title and the word count for each.
What information is available about Committed literature?
preguntas.respuesta: Committed literature (French: littérature engagée) can be defined as an approach of an author, poet, novelist, playwright or composer who commits their work to defend or assert an ethical, political, social, ideological or religious view, most often through their works but also can loosely be defined as being through their direct intervention as an "intellectual", in public affairs (Crowly, 2018).

preguntas.pregunta: Explain the topic of Interlingue literature in a few words.
preguntas.respuesta: Interlingue literature broadly encompasses the body of fiction and nonfiction work created or translated into Interlingue, a constructed language created by Edgar de Wahl.

preguntas.pregunta: What do we understand by Costumbrismo?
